titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/208

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=473 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:00:33.518659+00 | translation moa=474 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:00:31.848088+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Willelmus de Lilleselif salutem . Sciatis me dedisse . concessisse . et hac
presenti carta mea confirmasse deo et Ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in puram
et perpetuam Elemosinam duo tophta in occidentali parte de Hadigtun . Tenenda . habenda . possidenda . sicut eis
melius et vtilius uisum fuit . ita libere quiete . plenarie et honorifice sicut aliquam Elemosinam liberius . quietius . ple
nius et honorificentius tenent ac possident . Ita tamen quod de ipsis tophtis dabunt michi et heredibus meis annu
atim . quatuor denarios in nundinis de hadigtun . Ego autem et heredes mei manutenebimus et waranti
zabimus dictis monachis prefata duo tophta contra omnes homines . et adquietabimus de omni seruitio et de omnibus aliis rebus in perpetuum . His Testibus . Ricardo nano .Waltero de lindesei . Henrico filio Roberti de Ro
kesburc . Roberto de Bernoldebi . Alexandro de lillesclif . et Gaufrido fratre suo . Radulfo spendeluue preposito
de hadigtun . Willelmo clerico . Willelmo Russel

To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and to come, William of Lilliesleaf sends greeting. Know me to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in pure and perpetual alms, two tofts on the western side of Haddington; to be held, had, and possessed as seems best and most useful to them, as freely, quitly, fully and honourably as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitly, fully and honourably; so that they shall give me and my heirs four pence at the Haddington fair the from those tofts annually. I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks the aforementioned two tofts against all men, and discharge for ever every service and all other matters in perpetuity. With these witnesses: Richard le Naym; Walter of Lindsay; Henry son of Robert of Roxburgh; Robert of Barnoldby; Alexander of Lilliesleaf, and Geoffrey his brother; Ralph Spendlove, provost of Haddington; William the clerk; William Russell.
